content_markdown: The molar mass of magnesium is $24\text{ g}$. $12\text{ g}$ of magnesium contains the same number of particles as
vibe_explanation: The number of particles is proportional to the number of moles. For magnesium, $n = \frac{m}{M} = \frac{12\text{ g}}{24\text{ g/mol}} = 0.5\text{ mol}$. To have the same number of particles, we need $0.5\text{ mol}$ of carbon-12. Since the molar mass of carbon-12 is exactly $12\text{ g/mol}$, the required mass is $0.5\text{ mol} \times 12\text{ g/mol} = 6\text{ g}$.
